title: London: Lee Valley (Picketts Lock) & Greenwich

main_route.normalized_name: Picketts Lock, Lee Valley Leisure Centre, London
main_route.original_wording: Lee Valley Leisure Centre Picketts Lock
main_route.excerpt: At [?20] 1.45 we arrived at the Lee Valley Leisure Centre Picketts Lock.
After taking down the 2 tents, we left at 9.45.

excursions.label: Day trip: Oxford Circus shopping (Sat 25 Oct)

stops.excerpt: we were on the train bound for Oxford Circus.
Again we made a pilgrimage to Liberty's, Fabrics & lighting.
Then we spent nearly an hour in Hamleys.

excursions.label: Day trip: Greenwich river boat trip (Sun 26 Oct)

stops.excerpt: catching 2 buses to 7 Sisters & a tube train to Embankment.
A marvellous trip down the river on a boat in bright, bright sunshine. The boat stopped at Tower Bridge.
we circled in front of the Queen's House before docking. It was very beautiful. Greenwich
We went across into Greenwich Market - bustling, crowded.
Then we set off up the Hill to the Old Observatory. Most of us went around the Observatory.

stops.original_wording: "Cully Sark" 1868, last of the great Tea Clippers
stops.excerpt: the rest of us rushed down the hill to see the "Cully Sark" 1868, last of the great Tea Clippers which also carried wool.

excursions.label: Day trip: Imperial War Museum & Tate Gallery (Mon 27 Oct)

stops.excerpt: We first visited the Imperial War Museum.
we all finally met up at the Tate with Robert Upex for lunch.

stops.original_wording: the wonderful Clore Gallery full of Turners
stops.excerpt: looked around the wonderful Clore Gallery full of Turners.

excursions.label: Day trip: Madame Tussauds & Regent's Park (Tue 28 Oct)

stops.excerpt: We reached Madame Tussauds at 10.30. to meet Rob, Elizabeth + Cattarine Hungley in the queue. But the queue was 1¼ hours long so we left them to walk to Regents Park instead.
we left them to walk to Regents Park instead. We saw horrendous snakes, amusing chimpanzees + beautiful giraffes.

boundary_flags: mixed_end: true (diary continues into next trip after A_0225_L)
summary: A half-term family camping trip (all 7) to Picketts Lock, Lee Valley, used as a fixed base for four nights (25-28 October 1997). Day excursions covered central London shopping on Oxford Street, a Sunday river-boat trip to Greenwich with the Old Observatory and Cutty Sark, the Imperial War Museum and Tate Gallery on Monday, and Regent's Park Zoo on departure day Tuesday.
extraction_notes: Date 1997-10-25 confirmed: 'Sat October 25th' is a Saturday in 1997. No ferry or border crossings — entirely domestic UK trip. The outward journey route from Birmingham is narrated only briefly ('All 7 of us set off for London at 10.45'); the stops at St George's and the Priory en route were errand calls (dropping a leaflet, leaving a racket for restringing) not genuine tourist stops. The campsite also had an on-site cinema; several evening movies are mentioned but these are not excursion stops. 'Topkapi' on A_0225_L is truncated at the page boundary (mixed_end); it may have been a meal destination but the text cuts off — not included as a stop. The Clore Gallery is part of Tate Britain, recorded separately as that is how the diary names it. St. Alphege's Church is now usually spelled 'St Alfege's'.
